_meta.purpose: Arc 6 — Stripe SKU restructure — sandbox-agent scope expansion. THREE new Sids that land as a CUSTOMER-MANAGED policy named `LucielSandboxStripeScope` (ARN `arn:aws:iam::729005488042:policy/LucielSandboxStripeScope`) attached to IAM user `luciel-sandbox-agent` in account `729005488042` region `ca-central-1`. This file is the delta view of the new capability; the paste-ready form (Version+Statement wrapper, 646 chars) is at `LucielSandboxStripeScope.json`. The existing 11-Sid inline `LucielSandboxArc5MigrateScope` policy is byte-identical-unchanged; we did NOT append into it because doing so would have exceeded the IAM inline-user-policy 2048-char limit (2640 chars total vs 2048, 128.9%). Customer-managed shape was chosen over a second inline policy because it has its own version history, is independently revocable, can be reused on future principals (e.g. a CI runner), and gives a single canonical ARN for audit. Created by partner via IAM Console at 2026-05-23 6:26 PM EDT.
_meta.expansion_anchor: Arc 6 Commit 2 — Stripe Live wipe + Commit 4 — Stripe mint + SSM put
_meta.arc5_inline_policy_status: UNCHANGED (still 11 Sids, byte-identical to Arc 5 Commit 21 form)
_meta.verification_evidence: Four SSM probes executed at ≈22:26 UTC 2026-05-23 all PASSED: (1) get_parameter on stripe_price_intro_fee → price_* (30 chars); (2) get_parameter WithDecryption=True on stripe_secret_key → sk_live_* (107 chars); (3) put_parameter Type=SecureString to /luciel/production/stripe_arc6_iam_probe → 200 OK; (4) round-trip read on probe → exact match. Probe parameter cleanup deferred to Arc 6 Commit 11.
_meta.blast_radius_analysis: All three Sids resource-scope to `parameter/luciel/production/stripe_*` (a 9-key namespace, all Stripe-related: secret_key, webhook_secret, 7 Price IDs). The agent cannot read or write any other SSM parameter, including the database-url, magic-link-secret, JWT keys, or any non-Stripe production secret. KMS access is gated by `kms:ViaService=ssm.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com` — the AWS-managed SSM key can only be exercised through SSM API calls, not via direct KMS. Worst case on credential compromise: attacker can read Stripe Live secret key + the 7 Stripe Price IDs (already a known prod secret loaded into the running ECS container), and can write arbitrary values to those 9 SSM paths. Attacker CANNOT decrypt non-Stripe SSM SecureStrings, cannot rotate IAM, cannot affect database, cannot mutate ECS services or task definitions (those Sids remain unchanged), cannot affect any other account resource.
